topic: Part 6.14 κ Coupling Cluster — A Cross-Eclipse Vise (new section from EXP-301)
our_argument_summary: New Part 6.14 elevates the κ-coupling cluster (WIN-010, 011, 012, 013, 014, 058b, and PRED-TIER-1/2/3) into a single structural argument: if κ = 1.677 nT/µGal is a real coupling (as the dome publishes it), applying it to documented eclipse ΔB at 1999 European and 2009 East Asian total eclipses predicts contact-phase Δg of ~3-9 µGal at instrumented SG sites. The SGs that were continuously sampling those eclipses (Membach 1999; Chinese SG network 2009) recorded zero above the 0.01-1 µGal noise floor. The vise: either κ applies universally (in which case 1999/2009 SG nulls refute it) or κ has scope conditions (in which case PRED-TIER-3 has no derived target and collapses). Both horns lose at least one WIN/PRED. Eight-subsection structure matches operator plan; ~1,847 words; integrates cross-refs from WIN-013, WIN-014, WIN-058b detail prose.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes κ as a coupling constant in live-power dashboard (Domain 5) with predicted/observed 1.67/1.677 nT/µGal at 5% tolerance, derivation κ = 10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal = 1.677. The same derivation appears in raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-012 source description) and 05-model.txt (Domain 5 narrative). Section 6.14 accurately characterizes the derivation (two-eclipse single ratio between BOU 2017 magnetic and Mohe 1997 gravity) and accurately reports the load-bearing role across WIN-012 (definition), WIN-014 (validation appeal), live-power Domain (validation), and PRED-TIER-3 (derived target).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Live-power dashboard shows κ as a hardcoded predicted/observed pair (1.67/1.677, 5% tolerance, PASS); the "validation" is recomputing 10.9/6.5 from the same hardcoded numerator and denominator. The section correctly characterizes this as a display, not a real check.
data_sources_used: Mohe 1997 spring gravimeter (claimed via Wang et al. 2000)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome offers no published scope condition limiting κ to specific eclipse magnitudes, coverages, latitudes, bedrock types, or instrument classes. The section accurately notes this absence is what makes the vise close.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: CRITICAL TAG: The section asserts "monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67" — but the dome's published live-power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 73 in narrative, line 223 in domain output) and model page (raw-text/05-model.txt line 73) BOTH label EM-Gravity Coupling κ as Domain 5, not Domain 7. The dome's Domain 7 is "GPS Sagnac Verification." The same "Domain #7" error also appears verbatim in WIN-013.detail_evidence and WIN-014.detail_evidence (cross-checked) — EXP-301 propagated the same factual error to all three integration sites. A dome defender clicking through to live-power.html will see κ at Domain 5 and immediately point at the mismatch. The structural argument is unaffected (the dome HAS hardcoded κ; the domain number is cosmetic), but the credibility hit is real — "the curmudgeon's own review doesn't even cite the dome's public dashboard correctly." MODERATE finding; remediation is a 3-character patch in 3 places.

code_analysis_tags_note: code_analysis_tags do not apply to section-level reviews (those structural tags are per-WIN — monitoring/relabels_standard/post_hoc/derives_from_dome describe individual WIN automation, not aggregate-argument sections). Section 6.14 does, however, cross-reference structural tag patterns: it implicitly relies on WIN-012's derives_from_dome=false (κ is a fitted ratio, not derived from dome geometry) and WIN-058b's post_hoc=true (κ recomputed after the storm data was published). Those WIN-level tags should remain consistent with the section's framing; if a future curmudgeon re-tags WIN-012 or WIN-058b, the section may need a consistency pass.

holes_found.description: FACTUAL ERROR: Section asserts "monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67" in the first subsection ("What κ is and why the dome cannot abandon it"). The dome's own published live-power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) lists κ EM-Gravity Coupling as Domain 5; Domain 7 is GPS Sagnac Verification. Cross-checked the same error in WIN-013.detail_evidence and WIN-014.detail_evidence (both also say "Domain #7") — EXP-301 propagated the error to all three integration points. A dome defender comparing our text to live-power.html (or model.txt) immediately catches the mismatch. The vise argument is unaffected (the dome does hardcode κ; only the domain number is wrong), but the credibility cost on a high-visibility new section is real.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "Domain #7" with "Domain #5" in section 6.14 (1 occurrence), WIN-013.detail_evidence (1 occurrence), and WIN-014.detail_evidence (1 occurrence). Three-character patch each, totaling three edits across the κ-cluster integration. Decider should batch this with any other κ-cluster polish in a single self-applied patch.

holes_found.description: CITATION HYGIENE: In the "What past SG data actually shows" subsection, the parenthetical "Crossley et al. (2013) and the J9-Strasbourg intercomparison" is linked to DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4. The DOI string contains "022", consistent with a 2022 Pure and Applied Geophysics paper, but the in-text citation says "2013". Year mismatch between in-text citation and link destination. A dome defender will note that we appear to cite a 2013 paper while linking to a 2022 paper, suggesting we either (a) cite the wrong year, (b) cite the wrong paper, or (c) cite a 2022 paper as a proxy for 2013 content without saying so. None of those reads well on first inspection.
holes_found.recommendation: Either reword as "Crossley et al. (2013) report similar floors, as reviewed in [author] 2022" with the year/author of the actual 2022 paper, OR cite Crossley et al. 2013 directly with the original DOI (likely 10.1007/s00024-013-... or similar Pageoph 2013 DOI), OR drop the link and cite the 2013 paper by reference without hyperlink. As written, the year-link mismatch is a small but easy-to-find inconsistency on a load-bearing noise-floor citation.

holes_found.description: INCOMPLETE ENGAGEMENT WITH STRONGEST DOME REBUTTAL: The "What the dome could do" subsection lumps the "Chebychev low-pass argument that contact-phase transients are filtered out before Level-3 reduction" into a list of "post-hoc rescues constructed after the data refused to cooperate." This treatment is too dismissive of what is, methodologically, a real concern: standard SG processing pipelines (GGP/IGETS) do apply low-pass and bandpass filters (typically Butterworth or Chebychev, cutoffs in the 1-10 cycle/hour range) before publishing Level-3 residuals. A defender can legitimately say "of course the published Level-3 residuals show zero — contact-phase transients are seconds-to-minutes in duration and the standard pipeline attenuates them by an order of magnitude before publication." The section's vise still wins this exchange (because the raw 1-Hz SG records are available and not filtered, and Van Camp 2001 reports analyzing the raw record, not just Level-3 output), but the section needs to specify that the available evidence covers raw-record analysis, not just Level-3 publication. Without this specification, the argument is vulnerable to a one-line rebuttal that is technically true on its face.
holes_found.recommendation: Add 1-2 sentences in "What past SG data actually shows" or "What the dome could do" stating: SGs natively sample at 1-2 Hz; the Van Camp 2001 analysis includes minute-timescale residuals from raw records, not just standard Level-3 publication-quality residuals. The few-µGal contact-phase pulse predicted by κ would be visible in raw 1-Hz SG output regardless of any downstream low-pass filtering. A scope condition that says "κ applies only to signals attenuated below SG raw-record noise" is a different kind of carve-out (and a much harder one to publish credibly).

holes_found.description: WEAK CITATION FOR STORM CORALLARY NULL: The "storm corollary (WIN-058b)" subsection asserts "the published per-storm SG residual literature does not show systematic gravity pulses tracking storm magnetic perturbations through κ; the long-running SG records would have flagged such signals, and dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them." The first half is a defensible reading of the literature, but "dedicated SG-storm coupling searches have not found them" is asserted without a specific cite. A defender can request a citation; current text invites "prove the negative" pushback.
holes_found.recommendation: Either cite a specific SG-storm coupling search paper (e.g., Hinderer/Crossley group has published SG-geomagnetic correlation analyses) or soften to "no peer-reviewed analysis we are aware of has reported a κ-magnitude SG-storm coupling; WIN-058b's pending verdict reflects this gap and would resolve to Refuted by Data on any systematic survey that produced a null." The softer wording is honest about the literature-search bound; the cited wording is stronger if a paper actually exists.

holes_found.description: AGGRESSIVE PRECISION CLAIM: "continuously, across the relevant phases, at roughly 1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter" in "The vise" subsection. Spring gravimeters at minute timescales typically achieve 1-10 µGal; superconducting gravimeters achieve 0.01-1 µGal. The ratio is 10-1000x depending on which end of each range. "Roughly 1,000×" is the upper bound and slightly aggressive. Not wrong, but stated as if 1000x is the central estimate when 100x would be a fairer point estimate.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to "at 10-1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter" or "at 1-3 orders of magnitude better precision than the Mohe spring gravimeter." Preserves the qualitative point while not overclaiming.

stronger_arguments.description: SHOW THE PRED-TIER-3 DERIVATION EXPLICITLY: Section asserts "PRED-TIER-3 ... derives its 10-13 µGal SG-pulse target by dividing the predicted ΔB at named stations by κ" but never does the visible arithmetic. PRED-TIER-1 specifies ΔB = -17 to -21 nT umbra-locked. Applied to κ = 1.677: Δg = 17/1.677 to 21/1.677 = 10.1 to 12.5 µGal, almost exactly PRED-TIER-3's published 10-13 µGal target. Showing this two-step arithmetic in the "What κ is" subsection makes the vise structurally airtight on PRED-TIER-3: the prediction is literally κ × PRED-TIER-1, and any scope condition that exempts 1999/2009 must keep this relationship intact for August 2026 too — an extremely tight constraint.

INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY IN κ'S OWN DERIVATION: The dome's κ derivation already mixes a high-coverage denominator (Mohe 1997: essentially totality) with a sub-totality numerator (BOU 2017: ~92% local coverage at Boulder, well outside the path). Any post-hoc "totality-only" or "≥99% coverage" scope condition the dome might publish to exempt 1999 (95% at Membach) and 2009 from κ is internally inconsistent with how κ was constructed in the first place. The vise has a third horn: even an opportunistic scope condition cannot consistently exempt 1999 without retroactively invalidating the BOU 2017 numerator that the dome itself uses. This is a strong additional foreclosure on the "What the dome could do" rescue options.
sources: NASA eclipse coverage records for 11 Aug 1999 at 50.6°N (≈95% at Membach)

stronger_arguments.description: INTERMAGNET-DOURBES CORROBORATION (operator-flagged in EXP-301 as optional): The empirical_research_completed block flags pulling INTERMAGNET ΔB at Dourbes (DOU, ~50 km from Membach) for 11 Aug 1999 13:00-15:00 UT as optional follow-on. If/when done, this would replace the regional-network multi-citation chain (Strestík, Hvoždara, Curto) with a single per-station ΔB measurement at the eclipse-relevant station, tightening the κ-derived predicted Δg to a specific number per minute of the eclipse. Currently routed as a follow-on research item per analyst tag; remains valid.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that eclipse-induced magnetic perturbations DO exist (5-15 nT at central observatories during total eclipses is a real and reproducible signal documented in Strestík 2001, Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002, Curto et al. 2006, and the 39-eclipse ensemble in WIN-068). The dome also correctly identifies that the SG instruments around Mohe in 1997 reported some kind of gravity perturbation (Wang et al. 2000 published the original 6-7 µGal valley signal, even if Yang & Wang 2002 later disputed its eclipse-attribution). The cross-eclipse pairing of magnetic and gravity perturbations is a real intellectual move, not pseudoscience — eclipse-induced ionospheric and lower-atmospheric responses do produce small electromagnetic and (in principle) lower-mantle gravitational signatures, and an interdisciplinary coupling-constant hypothesis IS the kind of physics that warrants empirical testing (raw-text/05-model.txt:Domain 5; data/wins.json:WIN-010,WIN-011).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome makes three errors that defeat the kernel: (i) it derives κ from a single cross-eclipse, cross-instrument, cross-continent ratio with no co-located simultaneous measurement (data/wins.json:WIN-012 self-contradicted verdict explicitly cites this); (ii) it adopts the disputed 6.5 µGal Mohe denominator that the same lead observer later walked back (Yang & Wang 2002 in section 6.14 citation chain); (iii) it then treats κ as a deterministic constant (monitor.py Domain 5 hardcode at 5% tolerance, no scope conditions) rather than as a hypothesis to be tested at the next eclipses where SGs would record contact-phase signals. The actual SG observations at 1999 Membach (Van Camp 2001) and 2009 China network are the falsification test for κ-as-published, and they fired refute. Being directionally on the right scientific terrain (eclipse-coupled fields are studied; cross-domain coupling constants are a real thing) does not rescue a coupling that one's own published SG data refutes (data/sections.json:part6 section 6.14 "What past SG data actually shows"; data/wins.json:WIN-013 verdict Refuted by Data; data/wins.json:WIN-014 verdict Refuted by Data).

deeper_analysis: This section is a strong piece of structural reasoning. The eight-subsection plan from EXP-301 is executed faithfully; the vise framing is genuinely watertight under the dome's published treatment of κ as a deterministic constant; the cross-references to WIN-010/011/012/013/014/058b/025/068 and PRED-TIER-1/2/3 are all valid (verified against data/wins.json and data/predictions.json — every cited ID exists and the verdicts cited match current state). The TLDR is accurate and accessible. The 1,847-word count matches the ~1,800-word target. ADVOCATE MODE: I steelmanned the dome's strongest available defenses — (a) Chebychev/low-pass filtering of contact-phase transients, (b) bedrock conductivity scope conditions, (c) totality-vs-partial scope condition, (d) instrumental noise floor exemption, (e) κ-as-stochastic-envelope rather than deterministic constant — and the vise survives all five, though the (a) defense is the strongest standalone rebuttal and deserves explicit substantive engagement rather than "post-hoc rescue" framing (see holes_found item 3). CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY: The verdict reframes the section commissions for WIN-013 and WIN-014 (both now Refuted by Data per data/wins.json) are correctly supported by the section's empirical case; the section does not overstate. WIN-058b's Not Demonstrated status is preserved with a defensible "vise applies in principle, pending focused survey" framing that doesn't prejudge the verdict. QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION: κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769 ≈ 1.677 ✓. Δg = 5/1.677 = 2.98, Δg = 8/1.677 = 4.77 (cited 3-5 µGal) ✓. Δg = 10/1.677 = 5.96, Δg = 15/1.677 = 8.94 (cited 6-9 µGal) ✓. Δg = 5/1.677 = 2.98, Δg = 15/1.677 = 8.94 (cited 3-9 µGal for 2009 case) ✓. SG/floor ratios: 3/0.1 = 30, 6/0.01 = 600 (cited 30-600× for 1999) ✓; 3/0.1 = 30, 9/0.01 = 900 (cited 30-900× for 2009) ✓. PRED-TIER-1 (17-21 nT) ÷ κ = 10.1-12.5 µGal vs PRED-TIER-3 cited 10-13 µGal — confirms the κ-derivation claim and is the basis for stronger_argument #1. CITATION-CHAIN INTEGRITY: All in-text citations have anchors; most DOIs are well-formed. Van Camp 2001 DOI (10.1023/A:1013940928637) is reused from WIN-012 prose (carried forward, not introduced by this section) and is left in citations_unchecked — the DOI prefix is Kluwer (10.1023/A:) which is unusual for Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. (Elsevier), but transition-era 2001 cross-publisher DOI assignments do occur, so flagging only as unchecked rather than wrong. The Crossley/2022-DOI issue is a separate citation-hygiene hole. THE BIG STRUCTURAL FINDING — "Domain #7" vs "Domain #5" — is a cross-cutting error in EXP-301: identical wording appears in section 6.14, WIN-013 detail prose, AND WIN-014 detail prose. Decider should batch the patch across all three. Verdict: section is publishable as-is in terms of argument, needs ~5 minor patches (3 cosmetic, 2 substantive) before it ages.

PRED-TIER-1, PRED-TIER-2, PRED-TIER-3, PRED-KAPPA-001 — all exist in data/predictions.json with claims/verdicts matching the section's framing

DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 — DOI prefix (10.1023/A:) is Kluwer; PEPI is Elsevier — flagged but reused from pre-existing WIN-012 prose, not introduced by this section. summary_for_decider: SEC-6.14 is a strong new section — vise framing is airtight under the dome's published material, math checks out, WIN/PRED cross-references all verify. Five flagged items: (1) MODERATE — "monitor.py Domain #7" should be "Domain #5" per dome's own live-power.html and model.txt; THIS SAME ERROR ALSO APPEARS in WIN-013.detail_evidence and WIN-014.detail_evidence (both freshly integrated by EXP-301), so the patch is 3 sites for one bug. Recommend batching as a single self-applied minor patch across SEC-6.14 + WIN-013 + WIN-014. (2) MODERATE — Crossley 2013 in-text citation links to a 2022 DOI; reword or relink. (3) MODERATE — Chebychev low-pass argument deserves explicit engagement (raw 1-Hz SG records cover contact-phase signals), not dismissal as post-hoc rescue. (4) MINOR — WIN-058b storm-corollary null assertion lacks specific cite. (5) MINOR — "1,000× precision" overshoots; "10-1,000×" or "1-3 orders of magnitude better" is fairer. STRONGER ARGUMENTS to consider: (a) show PRED-TIER-3 = PRED-TIER-1/κ arithmetic visibly (17-21 nT ÷ 1.677 = 10.1-12.5 µGal ≈ PRED-TIER-3 10-13 µGal); (b) note that κ's own derivation mixes 92% BOU 2017 numerator with totality Mohe 1997 denominator, foreclosing any "totality-only" scope-condition rescue. Section is publishable as-is in terms of argument; recommend a single batch patch for the 5 items before it ages. queue_id 330 fully serviced. Cycle 1 first review; future reviews will inherit text_fingerprint baseline for change detection.
